operation: Decouple ring opacity from agent state — rings always on, state-blind identity markers
learnings: Ring = territory existence (constant). Emissive = activity state (variable). These are orthogonal. Never couple them.
When a magic number needs a comment, document the math that produces it, not just the intent.
Removing dead config fields (ringOpacityBase, ringOpacityAmplitude) is as important as the feature fix — dead fields are lies waiting to confuse.

Core/Scryer were always 'stable' because they have no TERRITORY_TO_AGENT entry → getAgentState returns 'online' → deriveBuildingState → 'stable'. The 4 agent territories went offline. Now rings never go offline.
agent_log: ARCHITECT: diagnosed root cause (offline state → ringOpacityBase 0.03), designed structural decoupling
BUILDER: 3 edits — interface cleanup, config cleanup + constants, useFrame ring block
COBALT pass 1 (Granny): flagged 0.65 magic number → fixed with inline math comment
COBALT pass 2 (Granny): ok:true, issues:[], all criteria pass
steps_completed: Removed ringOpacityBase + ringOpacityAmplitude from BuildingStateConfig interface
Removed those fields from all 3 config entries (offline, stable, working)
useFrame ring block: now reads constants only, 4 lines (was 8 tangled with cfg)
Selected opacity 0.65 documented with math rationale in comment
architectural_plaque: Ring = territory is present (always true). Emissive/label = current activity (variable). These are orthogonal signals. Decoupling them makes each legible on its own terms. All 6 territories breathe the same idle ring. Agent state drives the building, not the crown.
